perturbation.original_text: INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR CELEBRITY and CELEBRITY Personnel will act as an independent contractor in the performance of its duties under this Agreement. Accordingly, CELEBRITY will be responsible for payment of all federal, state, and local taxes on compensation paid under this Agreement, including income and social security taxes, unemployment insurance, and any other taxes due relative to Distributor's Personnel and any and all business license fees as may be required. This Agreement neither expressly nor impliedly creates a --- relationship of principal and agent, or employee and employer, between Distributor's Personnel and the COMPANY. Neither CELEBRITY nor CELEBRITY Personnel are authorized to enter into any agreements on behalf of the COMPANY. The COMPANY expressly retains the right to approve, in its sole discretion, each Asset Opportunity or Business Opportunity introduced by CELEBRITY, and to make all final decisions with respect to effecting a transaction on any Business Opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR CELEBRITY and CELEBRITY Personnel will act as an independent contractor in the performance of its duties under this Agreement. Accordingly, CELEBRITY will be responsible for payment of only federal income taxes on compensation paid under this Agreement. The COMPANY will handle all other taxes associated with the work, including social security and unemployment. This Agreement creates a relationship of principal and agent between CELEBRITY Personnel and the COMPANY. CELEBRITY Personnel are authorized to enter into agreements on behalf of the COMPANY with prior written approval. The COMPANY retains the right to approve, in its sole discretion, each Asset Opportunity or Business Opportunity introduced by CELEBRITY, and to make all final decisions with respect to effecting a transaction on any Business Opportunity.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the company handles all the employment taxes and that there is an agency relationship, the clause now directly contradicts the definition and responsibilities of an independent contractor versus an employee under federal law. Also, granting the celebrity personnel the authority to enter into agreements also contradicts the definition of an independent contractor.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Employee vs Independent Contractor Classification - IRS Guidelines
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification dictates that the company handles employment taxes, and the celebrity can enter agreements on behalf of the company, which is contrary to the definition of an independent contractor. Independent contractors are responsible for their own taxes (beyond federal income tax) and do not have the authority to act as agents for the company.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Independent contractor defined | Internal Revenue Service Skip to main content Independent contractor defined English Español 中文 (繁體) 한국어 Русский Tiếng Việt More In File Individuals Businesses and self-employed Business tax account Employer ID numbers Business taxes Business structures Operating a business Self-employed Independent contractors Gig workers Small business Large business Charities and nonprofits International taxpayers Governmental liaisons Federal, state and local governments Indian tribal governments Tax exempt bonds Taxpayer identification numbers (TIN) People such as doctors, dentists, veterinarians, lawyers, accountants, contractors, subcontractors, public stenographers, or auctioneers who are in an independent trade, business, or profession in which they offer their services to the general public are generally independent contractors. However, whether these people are independent contractors or employees depends on the facts in each case. The general rule is that an individual is an independent contractor if the person for whom the services are performed has the right to control or direct only the result of the work and not what will be done and how it will be done. If you are an independent contractor, then you are self-employed. The earnings of a person who is working as an independent contractor are subject to self-employment tax . To find out what your tax obligations are, visit the Self-Employed Individuals Tax Center . Generally, the person for whom the services are performed must report payments to independent contractors on Form 1099-NEC, Nonemployee Compensation , in box 1. You are not an independent contractor if you perform services that can be controlled by an employer (what will be done and how it will be done). This applies even if you are given freedom of action. What matters is that the employer has the legal right to control the details of how the services are performed. If an employer-employee relationship exists (regardless of what the relationship is called), then you are not an independent contractor and your earnings are generally not subject to self-employment tax. However, your earnings as an employee may be subject to FICA (social security and Medicare tax) and income tax withholding. Generally, earnings of an employee are reported on Form W-2, Wage and Tax Statement . Refer to the page, Independent Contractor (Self-Employed) or Employee? or Publication 15-A, Employer’s Supplement Tax Guide , for more information. Related Topics Reporting payments to independent contractor Estimated taxes Backup withholding Topic no. 307, Backup withholding Page Last Reviewed or Updated: 02-Jun-2025 Share Facebook Twitter Linkedin Print
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the definition of an independent contractor by stating the company handles employment taxes (beyond federal income tax) and granting the celebrity personnel the authority to enter into agreements on behalf of the company. The IRS snippet states that 'If you are an independent contractor, then you are self-employed. The earnings of a person who is working as an independent contractor are subject to self-employment tax'. Additionally, the snippet says 'You are not an independent contractor if you perform services that can be controlled by an employer (what will be done and how it will be done). This applies even if you are given freedom of action. What matters is that the employer has the legal right to control the details of how the services are performed.' By the company handling employment taxes and granting authority to enter agreements, the celebrity personnel is now essentially being treated as an employee instead of an independent contractor.

perturbation.original_text: TERMS FOR THE CELEBRITY Work with Jack Zufelt to develop a business strategy that can be implemented within 30 days of the signing of this agreement. (i) Identify marketing platform to recruit new distributors (ii) Develop a recruiting packet for new recruits that are duplicable with most tools coming from the corporate website and / or inventory. (iii) CELEBRITY will be available for phone conferences and meeting participating at any level deemed necessary by the COMPANY. (iv) CELEBRITY will be available for conference calls not to exceed five per month and at the discretion of his schedule. (v) The terms of this agreement will remain confidential between CELEBRITY and the COMPANY unless written permission is granted for release by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: TERMS FOR THE CELEBRITY Work with Jack Zufelt to develop a business strategy that can be implemented within 30 days of the signing of this agreement. (i) Identify marketing platform to recruit new distributors (ii) Develop a recruiting packet for new recruits that are duplicable with most tools coming from the corporate website and / or inventory. (iii) CELEBRITY will be available for phone conferences and meeting participating at any level deemed necessary by the CELEBRITY. (iv) CELEBRITY will be available for conference calls not to exceed five per month and at the discretion of his schedule. (v) The terms of this agreement may be released to the public by either CELEBRITY or the COMPANY. This is a promotional agreement and will not be held in strict confidence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'deemed necessary by the COMPANY' to 'deemed necessary by the CELEBRITY' directly contradicts the original intent and gives complete control over participation to the celebrity. Removing the confidentiality clause and stating that it can be released to the public undermines contract law regarding confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.law_explanation: By stating that the terms may be released, the modification contradicts typical contract law, where confidentiality clauses are legally binding. The modifications to the level of deemed necessary and removal of confidentiality are directly contradicting the laws.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: breach of contract | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you breach of contract A breach of contract occurs whenever a party who entered a contract fails to perform their promised obligations. Due to the frequency of breaches of contract, a robust body of law has grown to resolve the ensuing disputes. The overarching goal of contract law is to place the harmed party in the same economic position they would have been in had no breach of contract occurred. As a result, the default remedy available for a breach of contract is monetary damages . Generally, these damages are limited to what is listed in the contract and, unlike damages from tort cases, courts do not award punitive damages for breaches of contract. For example, if a party agrees to pay $50,000 to have their house painted but is only willing to hand over $10,000 once the painting is complete, the court will award the painters $40,000 in damages. This hesitancy to award punitive damages is due to the theory of efficient breach which argues that breaching contracts and paying damages is sometimes economically beneficial for society as a whole. Nonetheless, in specific circumstances, a party may successfully recover more money than initially contracted for under the doctrine of reliance damages . Under this doctrine , a party who reasonably relied upon a contract that was later breached can be granted compensation for the reasonable expenses they incurred due to that reliance. For example, a party who purchases lifeguard equipment in reliance upon a pool construction contract’s fulfillment may be able to recover the costs of the lifeguard equipment in the event of a breach. Reliance damages are based upon the principle of promissory estoppel , and granting them is subject to the court’s discretion. That said, parties harmed by a breach of contract have a duty to mitigate that harm. For example, before they could recover, the aforementioned lifeguard equipment buyer must first attempt to resell the equipment to a new buyer. Failure to satisfy the duty to mitigate will result in an inability to recover damages. In scenarios where damages are insufficient, a court may instead award specific performance . Under the specific performance remedy, the breaching party must attempt to fulfill the terms of the contract as best as possible. Specific performance, however, is generally only awarded when dealing with one-of-a-kind assets like real estate . Parties wishing to contract around the above remedies can do so through the use of liquidated damages provisions. These provisions establish in advance how much money a breaching party must pay and sidestep the expensive and time-consuming process of determining the actual damage caused by the breach. While liquidated damages clauses are generally allowed, a court may strike one down if the clause appears to be proxying for punitive damages or if the terms of the clause are unconscionable . [Last reviewed in June of 2022 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE commercial activities THE LEGAL PROCESS courts legal practice/ethics wex articles business law contracts wex definitions business sectors civil procedure commercial transactions courts and procedure legal education and practice legal theory money and financial problems Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modification removes the confidentiality clause entirely, stating that the agreement 'may be released to the public by either CELEBRITY or the COMPANY.' This directly contradicts the common law expectation that confidentiality agreements are legally binding, as supported by the Law Explanation that such modifications 'contradict typical contract law.' The provided snippet defines a breach of contract as when 'a party who entered a contract fails to perform their promised obligations,' and eliminating the confidentiality clause fundamentally alters the obligations agreed upon.

perturbation.original_text: (iv) The terms of this agreement will remain confidential between CELEBRITY and the COMPANY unless written permission is granted for release by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: (iv) The terms of this agreement will remain confidential between CELEBRITY and the COMPANY; however, the company can unilaterally decide to release the terms of this agreement to its partners.
perturbation.explanation: This modification undermines the mutual agreement aspect of a confidentiality clause. By allowing one party to unilaterally release the information, it effectively nullifies the protection afforded by the confidentiality agreement. This creates a significant imbalance and contradicts the principle of mutual consent required for legally binding contracts.
perturbation.law_explanation: By providing unilateral power to the company in releasing private information, this negates the confidential agreement originally created.

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction because it allows the COMPANY to unilaterally release confidential information, which undermines the mutual agreement initially established in the original text. This directly contradicts the principle that a breach of contract occurs when a party fails to perform their promised obligations, as detailed in Scraped Law Snippet 2. By granting the company unilateral power, the confidentiality agreement's binding nature is compromised, potentially leading to a breach if the company releases information without the CELEBRITY's consent. The original agreement ensured mutual consent for release, a fundamental aspect of contractual obligations which is weakened by the changed text.
